title: AGENTS.md
text: erglm provides estimation tools exposure-response models based glm(): model fitting (erglm_model()), prediction confidence intervals (erglm_predict()), stepwise covariate modelling (erglm_scm_forward() / erglm_scm_backward() / erglm_scm_history()), simulation (erglm_simulator(), erglm_vpc_sim()). erglm_model() takes family argument, defaulting stats::gaussian() (matching glm()’s default); binomial, poisson, gaussian, Gamma tested officially supported end end (fitting, prediction, SCM significance testing, VPC simulation). glm() families work generic mechanisms erglm_predict()/erglm_simulator() aren’t covered SCM’s test selection VPC’s noise draws. package previously called erlr (logistic-regression-, lr_*-prefixed functions); renamed erglm generalised arbitrary glm() families, per PLAN.md step 6. lr_* deprecated aliases – clean-break rename, since package predates CRAN release external users. deliberately contains plotting code. model-agnostic mini-language visualise exposure-response models (including fitted ), see companion package erplots. erglm interoperates erplots implementing er_predict() / er_simulate() / er_summary() generics erplots defines, registered lazily load time (see R/er-methods.R) – erglm hard dependency erplots plotting packages (ggplot2, patchwork). Known follow-(yet done): companion erplots repo still references old package/function names (erlr::lr_model(), erlr::lr_data) DESCRIPTION Suggests, test helpers, vignette article – needs corresponding update rename published, erlr-dependent tests/vignette break.

title: AGENTS.md
text: See PLAN.md history generalisation/rename project. family generalisation (steps 1-5), erglm rename (step 6), manual infrastructure follow-ups (renaming GitHub repo djnavarro/erlr -> djnavarro/erglm, repointing erglm.djnavarro.net pkgdown domain/DNS) now done. remaining item companion erplots repo update noted .

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/erglm-core.R – erglm_model(), erglm_predict(), erglm_simulator() closure factory, shared .erglm_simulate_draws() helper (used erglm_vpc_sim() , via erplots, spaghetti-style plots). R/erglm-scm.R – forward/backward stepwise covariate modelling. R/erglm-vpc.R – erglm_vpc_sim(). R/erglm-family.R – shared family-dispatch helpers used SCM VPC: .erglm_default_test() (picks "Chisq" vs "F" stats::anova() based family’s dispersion behaviour) .erglm_draw_response() (family-specific residual noise draws VPC simulation; binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma , errors informatively otherwise). R/erglm-data.R – synthetic erglm_data example dataset. binary (ae1, ae2), count (ae_count), continuous (biomarker_change), positive/right-skewed continuous (ae_duration) response columns, demonstrating binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma models respectively. R/er-methods.R – erplots interoperability: S3 methods er_predict()/er_simulate()/er_summary(), plus lazy registration via .onLoad() (vendored s3_register() – standard pattern optional cross-package S3 methods). er_summary.erglm_model()’s p-value extraction family-generic (matches Pr(>|z|) Pr(>|t|) pattern). R/utils-helpers.R, R/utils-global.R – small internal helpers globalVariables() declarations NSE. .as_erglm() records fitted model’s actual family (stats::family(mod)$family) mod$erglm$type.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Document roxygen2 (devtools::document()); Markdown roxygen enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)). Run tests devtools::test(); full checks devtools::check(). package check cleanly (0 errors/warnings/notes). Tests live tests/testthat/, roughly one file per R/ source file. tests/testthat/test-er-methods.R exercises interop erplots skipped erplots isn’t installed. Vignettes/articles live vignettes/articles/ built pkgdown site, shipped package (see .Rbuildignore).

title: AGENTS.md
text: Use base R pipe (|>), magrittr pipe. Follow existing tidyverse-style conventions (dplyr/tibble/rlang) already used throughout. Public functions prefixed erglm_; internal helpers prefixed .erglm_ (, couple package-wide utilities like .pick_seed(), prefix ). Model objects plain glm objects extra erglm_model class (name constructor function erglm_model(), matching base-R idiom lm()/class "lm") internal $erglm list package-specific metadata (e.g. SCM history) – see .as_erglm(). Don’t add plotting code – belongs erplots.

title: erglm development plan
text: document tracks scoped-future development erglm (formerly erlr, renamed per step 6 ). changelog; see NEWS.md one exists. Items proposals reviewed implementation, committed designs; sections kept historical record generalisation/rename project even though package since moved erlr/lr_* names describe.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erglm development plan
text: Since plotting mini-language moved erplots, erlr fairly thin wrapper around glm(family = binomial(link = "logit")). underlying machinery (prediction CIs, parameter-uncertainty simulation, stepwise covariate modelling) actually specific logistic regression – work, minor changes, glm() family. Generalising natural next step, happen initial CRAN release (avoid disruptive rename afterwards). package renamed erglm point.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erglm development plan
text: lr_predict() already computes CIs link scale using stats::family(object)$linkinv, hardcoded logit/inverse-logit. work unchanged glm family. lr_simulator() builds model matrix (response-stripped) formula applies stats::family(object)$linkinv – also already family-agnostic.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erglm development plan
text: lr_model(): currently hardcodes family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"). Needs family argument; see decision (2) default renamed. .as_erlr(): hardcodes mod$erlr$type <- "logistic". record actual family (stats::family(mod)$family) instead. er_summary.erlr_glm() (R/er-methods.R): extracts p-value column "Pr(>|z|)" summary(model)$coefficients. column "Pr(>|t|)" families estimated dispersion parameter (gaussian, Gamma, inverse.gaussian, quasi*). Needs match "Pr(" column pattern rather exact name. .lr_anova_p() (stepwise covariate modelling): uses stats::anova(mod1, mod2) reads Pr(>Chi). likelihood-ratio chi-squared test appropriate families known dispersion (binomial, Poisson) families estimated dispersion parameter (gaussian, Gamma), F-test standard choice (stats::anova(mod1, mod2, test = "F")). SCM needs pick test based family, expose argument, read resulting column name generically ("Pr(>Chi)" vs "Pr(>F)"). lr_vpc_sim() / .lr_simulate_draws(): currently returns expected response sampled parameters (fit_resp) “simulated” value – reasonable shortcut 0/1 response (’s simulated probability), continuous count response proper VPC typically needs full draw including residual/dispersion noise (e.g. rnorm(n, mean = fit, sd = sigma) gaussian, rpois(n, lambda = fit) Poisson), just conditional mean. needs family-dispatched noise-generation step, likely via internal generic keyed family(model)$family. Naming: functions prefixed lr_ (logistic regression specific). Proposed rename scheme (open discussion): lr_model() → glm_model(), lr_predict() → glm_predict(), lr_simulator() → glm_simulator(), lr_scm_*() → glm_scm_*(), lr_vpc_sim() → glm_vpc_sim(). Class erlr_glm → erglm_model (similar). erplots dispatches purely generic name plus whatever class erlr/erglm chooses register, rename entirely internal package – coordination needed erplots beyond updating registerS3method() calls R/er-methods.R. Example data: lr_data binary (ae1/ae2) responses. Demonstrating gaussian/Poisson families well benefit adding continuous /count response column (also useful erplots’ continuous-response work, see PLAN.md).

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erglm development plan
text: questions originally left open; now working recommendation implementation isn’t blocked, still debate new information changes calculus. Rename timing. family generalisation erglm rename two separate steps, one PR: generalise behaviour first (existing erlr name), get reviewed tested, rename final, purely mechanical pass right CRAN submission. Rationale: keeps behavioural review separate large, low-risk find--replace diff, means generalisation decision can revisited without re-rename. (matches existing step ordering , rename last.) Default family. renamed, glm_model() default family = stats::gaussian() – .e. match stats::glm()’s default – rather keeping binomial-logit default. package ’s longer logistic-regression-specific shouldn’t quietly special-case binary responses; users fitting logistic models pass family = binomial() explicitly, base glm(). predictable choice anyone coming base R, cost breaking change existing lr_model() callers – acceptable given rename already breaks call sites. v1 family scope. Explicitly support test four families cover common exposure-response use cases: binomial (binary AE), poisson (count AE), gaussian (continuous biomarker), Gamma (skewed continuous/time-based endpoints). families (inverse.gaussian, quasi-families, etc.) work generic mechanisms “untested, officially supported” someone actually needs one – don’t build speculative test matrix families current use case. SCM test selection. Automatic default, escape hatch: pick test family’s dispersion behaviour ("Chisq" binomial/poisson, known dispersion; "F" gaussian/Gamma/inverse.gaussian/quasi*, estimated dispersion), expose test = c("auto", "Chisq", "F") forward/backward SCM functions users can override . follows convention car::Anova() similar tools use, avoids silently giving wrong-flavoured p-values less common families. VPC noise-model dispatch. Implement , rather punting documented limitation – VPCs core diagnostic domain, “expectation ” VPC materially weaker continuous/count responses (understates predictive uncertainty ignoring residual noise entirely). Scope initial implementation four families (3): Bernoulli/probability draws binomial, rpois() poisson, rnorm() (using estimated residual SD) gaussian, rgamma() (using estimated shape/dispersion) Gamma. Families outside set raise informative error rather silently falling back expectation-shortcut.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erglm development plan
text: Add family argument lr_model(); generalise .as_erlr(). Done. lr_model() gained family argument (still defaulting binomial(link = "logit") backward compatibility, per decision 2 – switch gaussian() default happens rename time). .as_erlr() now records stats::family(mod)$family instead hardcoded "logistic". Generalise er_summary.erlr_glm()’s p-value column lookup. Done, now matches ^Pr\( coefficient table’s column names. Generalise SCM’s test statistic selection. Done. lr_scm_forward() / lr_scm_backward() gained test = c("auto", "Chisq", "F") argument (see decision 4); .lr_anova_p() picks test automatically family’s dispersion behaviour (R/lr-family.R::.lr_default_test()) reads p-value column generically. Also fixed latent bug .lr_add_term()/ .lr_remove_term(), previously refit via lr_model() without passing original model’s family (SCM non-default family silently refit binomial-logit). Design implement family-dispatched VPC noise generation. Done, per decision 5, scoped binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma (R/lr-family.R::.lr_draw_response()); families raise informative error lr_vpc_sim() rather silently falling back expectation-draws. .lr_simulate_draws() unchanged (still expectation-, since ’s shared er_simulate.erlr_glm()/spaghetti plots, want smooth expectation curves) – noise draw applied lr_vpc_sim(), top .lr_simulate_draws()’s output. Expand lr_data (add second example dataset) continuous/count responses; expand tests across families. Done. Added ae_count (poisson), biomarker_change (gaussian), ae_duration (Gamma) columns lr_data, generated appending new draws existing generator code id/sex/…/ae1/ ae2 unchanged bit--bit seed. Tests extended across four families test-lr-core.R, test-lr-scm.R, test-lr-vpc.R, test-er-methods.R; vignette articles model.Rmd simulate.Rmd gained non-binomial worked examples. Execute erglm rename (package name, exported function names, class names, DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE/pkgdown/README/vignettes, GitHub repo). Done (-repo changes). Final naming scheme used: package erlr → erglm; lr_model() → erglm_model() (default family now stats::gaussian(), per decision 2); lr_predict() → erglm_predict(); lr_simulator() → erglm_simulator(); lr_scm_forward()/lr_scm_backward()/lr_scm_history() → erglm_scm_forward()/erglm_scm_backward()/erglm_scm_history(); lr_vpc_sim() → erglm_vpc_sim(); dataset lr_data → erglm_data; class erlr_glm → erglm_model (name constructor function, matching lm()/"lm" base-R idiom); internal .lr_* helpers → .erglm_*. Clean break, deprecated lr_* aliases. Version bumped 0.2.0.9000. GitHub repo (djnavarro/erlr → djnavarro/erglm) since renamed, erglm.djnavarro.net pkgdown custom domain/DNS live (HTTPS certificate approved) – manual/infrastructure follow-ups original rename plan complete. local clone directory also renamed erlr erglm consistency (filesystem-level, outside git). Still done (tracked separately): updating companion erplots repo, still references erlr::lr_model()/ erlr::lr_data DESCRIPTION (Suggests: erlr), tests/testthat/helper-data.R, vignettes/articles/plot.Rmd. break erglm published new name; needs follow-PR repo.

title: Modelling
text: Creating model:
code: mod <- erglm_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Modelling
text: erglm_predict() function produces model predictions: confidence level can adjusted using conf_level argument

title: Modelling
text: two functions control SCM regression, erglm_scm_forward() erglm_scm_backward(): extract log, use erglm_scm_history():
code: base_mod <- erglm_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = erglm_data, family = binomial()) candidates <- c("sex", "dose", "weight", "age")  final_mod <- base_mod |>    erglm_scm_forward(candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = 3425) |>    erglm_scm_backward(candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = 9821)  final_mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4 erglm_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~weight     ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         1       3 forward    add    ~age        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 5         1       4 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

title: Modelling
text: erglm_data also includes count response (ae_count), continuous response (biomarker_change), right-skewed positive continuous response (ae_duration), demonstrating poisson, gaussian, Gamma models respectively: erglm_predict() erglm_simulator() work unchanged, since operate link scale generically via stats::family(object)$linkinv: Stepwise covariate modelling also generalises: default erglm_scm_forward()/erglm_scm_backward() pick likelihood-ratio chi-squared test poisson () binomial models, F-test gaussian/Gamma models, matching stats::anova()’s test argument. can overridden test argument needed.
code: mod_pois <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = poisson()) mod_pois #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss      sexMale   #>   -0.930026     0.001053    -0.172638   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  297 Residual #> Null Deviance:       868.8  #> Residual Deviance: 272.4     AIC: 712.6 mod_pois |>    erglm_predict(newdata = tibble(aucss = seq(0, 3000, by = 500), sex = "Female")) #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>   aucss sex    fit_link se_link fit_resp ci_lower ci_upper #>   <dbl> <chr>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1     0 Female   -0.930  0.104     0.395    0.322    0.484 #> 2   500 Female   -0.404  0.0888    0.668    0.561    0.795 #> 3  1000 Female    0.123  0.0769    1.13     0.973    1.31  #> 4  1500 Female    0.650  0.0696    1.91     1.67     2.19  #> 5  2000 Female    1.18   0.0684    3.24     2.84     3.71  #> 6  2500 Female    1.70   0.0736    5.49     4.75     6.34  #> 7  3000 Female    2.23   0.0841    9.29     7.88    11.0

title: Simulation
text: erglm_vpc_sim() draws simulated response values reflect parameter uncertainty (resampling coefficients model’s asymptotic sampling distribution) , family-appropriately, residual noise around resulting expectation: noise model family-specific: Bernoulli draws binomial, Poisson draws poisson, normal draws gaussian, gamma draws Gamma. example, Poisson response: glm() families aren’t currently supported erglm_vpc_sim() raise informative error rather silently falling back expectation-draw.
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod, nsim = 20, seed = 1234) sim #> # A tibble: 6,000 × 5 #>      ae2 aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>    <int> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1     0  673. Male        1      1 #>  2     1 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3     0    0  Female      3      1 #>  4     1 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5     0  377. Male        5      1 #>  6     0  327. Female      6      1 #>  7     0    0  Male        7      1 #>  8     1 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9     0    0  Male        9      1 #> 10     0  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 5,990 more rows mod_pois <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = poisson()) sim_pois <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_pois, nsim = 20, seed = 1234) sim_pois #> # A tibble: 6,000 × 5 #>    ae_count aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>       <int> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1        2  673. Male        1      1 #>  2       11 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3        0    0  Female      3      1 #>  4        0 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5        0  377. Male        5      1 #>  6        0  327. Female      6      1 #>  7        0    0  Male        7      1 #>  8        0 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9        1    0  Male        9      1 #> 10        0  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 5,990 more rows

code: @Manual{,   title = {erglm: Exposure-Response Tools for GLM-Based Models},   author = {Danielle Navarro},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/djnavarro/erglm}, }

title: Exposure-Response Tools for GLM-Based Models
text: Provides estimation tools exposure-response models based glm(). mostly intended convenience package: core tools wrappers around glm(), officially tested supported binomial, poisson, gaussian, Gamma families. plotting exposure-response models (including fitted erglm), see companion package erplots, supplies model-agnostic mini-language building exposure-response plots.

title: Exposure-Response Tools for GLM-Based Models
text: can install development version erglm like :
code: pak::pak("djnavarro/erglm")

title: Exposure-Response Tools for GLM-Based Models
code: library(erglm) library(tibble)  erglm_data #> # A tibble: 300 × 13 #>       id sex      age weight  dose treatment aucss cmaxss   ae1   ae2 ae_count #>    <int> <fct>  <int>  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <int> #>  1     1 Male      35     79   200 Drug       673.   97.3     0     1        1 #>  2     2 Female    22     58   200 Drug      2806.  301.      1     1        6 #>  3     3 Female    28     58     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0        1 #>  4     4 Female    18     57   100 Drug      1169.  198.      1     1        0 #>  5     5 Male      28     77   100 Drug       377.   51.4     0     0        0 #>  6     6 Female    19     76   200 Drug       327.   25.4     1     0        0 #>  7     7 Male      30     70     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0        0 #>  8     8 Female    34     60   100 Drug      1208.  133.      1     1        1 #>  9     9 Male      21     89     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0        0 #> 10    10 Female    34     56   200 Drug       254.   31.0     0     0        1 #> # ℹ 290 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: biomarker_change <dbl>, ae_duration <dbl>  mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Exposure-Response Tools for GLM-Based Models
code: mod1 <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod2 <- erglm_scm_backward(mod1, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 7116 erglm_scm_history(mod2) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

title: Fit an exposure-response model based on glm() — erglm_model
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:	    402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4 	AIC: 197.4  # other glm() families are also supported mod_pois <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) mod_pois #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = family, data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.003955     0.001044   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:	    868.8  #> Residual Deviance: 275.6 	AIC: 713.8

what: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
title: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
text: Stepwise covariate modelling exposure-response models

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
code: erglm_scm_forward(   mod,   candidates,   threshold = 0.01,   test = c("auto", "Chisq", "F"),   seed = NULL )  erglm_scm_backward(   mod,   candidates,   threshold = 0.001,   test = c("auto", "Chisq", "F"),   seed = NULL )  erglm_scm_history(mod)

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
text: mod erglm model object candidates Character vector list candidate terms threshold Threshold test test significance test use comparing nested models. "auto" (default) picks likelihood-ratio chi-squared test ("Chisq") families known dispersion (binomial, poisson) F-test ("F") families estimated dispersion parameter (gaussian, Gamma, inverse.gaussian, quasi*), matching stats::anova()'s test argument. Set explicitly override. seed Optional seed control order term tests

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
text: erglm_scm_forward() erglm_scm_backward(), updated erglm model returned, SCM history log updated internally. erglm_scm_history(), data frame returned containing SCM history log

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for exposure-response models — erglm_scm
code: mod0 <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod1 <- erglm_scm_forward(mod0, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 6292 erglm_scm_history(mod1) #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>  mod2 <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod3 <- erglm_scm_backward(mod2, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 6526 erglm_scm_history(mod3) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

what: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
title: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
text: Simulate exposure-response model

title: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
code: erglm_simulator(object)

title: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
text: object erglm model, returned erglm_model()

title: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
text: function arguments param, data, type. param argument vector coefficients data argument data frame tibble type argument string indicating type prediction generate (defaults "response") Takes fitted glm object input returns function evaluate underlying structural model user-specified parameters data (e.g., VPCs counterfactual simulation scenarios). Uses stats::family(object)$linkinv, works glm() family, just binomial/logistic models; tested binomial, poisson, gaussian, Gamma families.

title: Simulate from an exposure-response model — erglm_simulator
code: mod1 <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) par1 <- coef(mod1) mod1_sim <- erglm_simulator(mod1)  # no counterfactuals p1 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = erglm_data)  p2 <- unname(predict(mod1, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the data set erglm_data2 <- erglm_data[1:20, ] p3 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = erglm_data2)  p4 <- unname(predict(mod1, newdata = erglm_data2, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the parameters par2 <- par1 int1 <- par1["(Intercept)"] par2["(Intercept)"] <- 0 p5 <- mod1_sim(param = par2, data = erglm_data)

what: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
text: VPC simulations exposure-response models

title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
code: erglm_vpc_sim(object, nsim = 100, seed = NULL)

title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
text: object erglm model, returned erglm_model() nsim Number replicates seed RNG state

title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
text: data frame tibble. Contains one row per observation per simulated replicate (identified sim_id column), response variable replaced simulated value parameter uncertainty.

title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
text: replicate, parameter uncertainty reflected sampling coefficients model's asymptotic sampling distribution computing expected response coefficients; family-appropriate residual noise added top expectation produce full predictive draw (Bernoulli draws binomial, Poisson draws poisson, normal draws gaussian, gamma draws Gamma). dispersion parameter used noise single point estimate (summary(object)$dispersion), resampled per replicate. glm() families currently supported raise error. visualise result (e.g. VPC-style plot comparing observed simulated response rates), see erplots::er_vpc_plot().

title: VPC simulations for exposure-response models — erglm_vpc_sim
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod) #> Using seed = 7603 sim #> # A tibble: 30,000 × 5 #>      ae2 aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>    <int> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1     0  673. Male        1      1 #>  2     1 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3     0    0  Female      3      1 #>  4     1 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5     0  377. Male        5      1 #>  6     0  327. Female      6      1 #>  7     0    0  Male        7      1 #>  8     0 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9     0    0  Male        9      1 #> 10     0  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows  mod_pois <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = poisson()) erglm_vpc_sim(mod_pois) #> Using seed = 9053 #> # A tibble: 30,000 × 5 #>    ae_count aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>       <int> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1        0  673. Male        1      1 #>  2        5 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3        1    0  Female      3      1 #>  4        1 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5        1  377. Male        5      1 #>  6        0  327. Female      6      1 #>  7        0    0  Male        7      1 #>  8        1 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9        1    0  Male        9      1 #> 10        1  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows
